source: # Manual-import seed for Senegal fertilizer consumption + subsidy allocation.
# WHY THIS FILE EXISTS:
#   Senegal's DAPSA (Direction de l'Analyse
source_record_id: de la Prévision et des Statistiques

source: #   Agricoles) publishes the Enquête Agricole Annuelle (EAA) and operates the
#   e-Subvention program. All public artifacts are PDFs at
#   https://www.dapsa.gouv.sn/sites/default/files/publications/*.pdf —
#   no XLSX/CSV export. Senegal is NOT covered by VIFAA retail prices today
#   (sparse entries; SEN is in the priority pull list but data is thin).
#   See ../canonical/AFRICA_DATA_SOURCES.md for the full audit.
# HOW TO POPULATE:
#   1. Pull the latest EAA report from https://www.dapsa.gouv.sn/toutes-les-publications
#      (typically annual; covers the previous agricultural campaign).
#   2. The fertilizer section reports either national tonnes by N/P2O5/K2O or
#      subsidy tonnages by crop (peanut/arachide
#   3. For CONSUMPTION rows: emit one row per (nutrient
#      level (state_or_region = NULL).
#   4. For SUBSIDY-ALLOCATION rows: these belong in fertilizer_price
#      fertilizer_use. Add to a separate manual_import_sen_subsidy.csv when
#      that becomes a priority.
#   5. Commit with the source PDF URL in the commit message.
# SCHEMA (canonical fertilizer_use):
#   source            = 'manual_import_sen_consumption'
#   source_record_id  = composite
#   country_iso3      = 'SEN'
#   country_name      = 'Senegal'
#   state_or_region   = region name (e.g. 'Dakar'
#   year              = calendar year of effect (use start year of agricultural-year ranges)
#   nutrient          = 'N' | 'P2O5' | 'K2O' | 'total'
#   total_tonnes      = consumption in metric tonnes
#   kg_per_ha_arable  = optional; NULL if only tonnes reported
#   arable_land_ha    = optional
#   source_url        = direct URL to the EAA PDF
#   retrieved_at      = ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of extraction
#   review_flags      = always 'manual_import'; add caveats like 'EAA_2022_23' or 'national_only'
# CURRENT STATE: header-only. Awaiting first analyst extraction.
